Service identification.content: Service TAM Oracle Data Integrator (ODI) Unique ID PRD.0.L.138 Service Model Technical Application Management (TAM) This service is dependant of other layers (IaaS and/or PaaS) which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the ‘infrastructure up to the technical application’ management. These obligatority services are called the ‘Platform’.

Technical Application Management.content: The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage an Oracle Data Integrator (ODI) application (The Application) from a technical perspective. Together with the underlaying obligatory Platform services, Cegeka unburdens the customer with all technical aspects from infrastructure up to the technical Application. The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters. The Customer is responsible for the setup and management of the actual ELT tasks.

The Application.content: Oracle Data Integrator (ODI) is a software product that allows you to perform ELT (Extract, Load, Transform) functions between data sources (databases and other files). Oracle Data Integrator Enterprise Edition delivers high-performance data movement and transformation among enterprise platforms with its open and integrated ELT architecture and extended support for Big Data. Each instance (e.g. IVL, CIVL,) has an ODI agent running on the central environment.

Standard Service features.content: Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the ODI technology in order to perform the Service. Cegeka advises in the setup of the underlaying platform in order to set it up according to the technology requirements of the Application. This may be different for each Customer environment (DTAP - Development, Test, Acceptance, Production and it’s variants). High availability setup: this setup advise includes also the setup of HA (High Available) environments where needed. Cegeka will advise a cost effective solution within the context  of the available platform(s) of the Customer and the best practices of the Application. Cegeka performs and maintains the technical installation of the Application on the agreed platform ensuring its correct working. Technical setup is documented on operational level and is available to the Customer. Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Application and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows. Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Application. Automation is applied where possible. Proactive management Housekeeping includes following regular checks: Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA. Patching The service includes a maximum of 2 planned patch cycles (per environment) per year for the managed Application (inclusive the system database(s) if applicable). Patching is done in accordance to the operational Patch Plan. Additional patching can be done on RFC basis. Patching of the underlying platform is part of the Service of that platform. Life Cycle Management Cegeka monitors the Technology version of the service and notifies the Customer in accordance with the agreed LCM policy (see cross-functional Services) when used versions are subject to end-of-support or end-of-life. Appropriate actions (updates, replacement or other actions) are planned in accordance with agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis in case of major updates. Patches and minor updates are included in the service cost as part of ‘Patching’. Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Application including the ODI agents on all Customer environments. Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service and are based on evaluation of incidents, problem management or other suggestions. The underlaying platform is also monitored according to the related Service Description of the platform. Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management. Management of technical users The management of technical users (for engineers) is done by Cegeka in agreement with the Customer and in accordance to the Customer IAM policies. Detection of security incidents The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders. SIEM Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request. Underlaying Platform hardening The underlaying platform and OS will be hardened as part of and according to the Service Description of the related Services. The operational Backup Plan contains the information on frequency and retention of the backups in accordance to the Customer backup policy. Restores can be done both on file and on full system level. These can be requested via the regular RFC process. Duration of a restore is dependant of the request (file or full; size of the item(s) to be restored). Application settings are subject to the backup of the underlaying platform. The backup of the ODI repository database is subject of the backup of this TAM service. The underlaying platform and OS will be hardened as part of and according to the Service Description of the related Services. Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals). Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals). The functional usage and monitoring of the Application is the responsibility of the Customer.

Optional Service features.content: This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features. The following optional features are possible with the Service:

Requests.content: Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). request type Description Lead time Service Requests Restart an ODI agent 1 working hour Normal Changes Any other request. Examples: technical support and consultancy on the functional use or debugging of the Application functionality Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.

Out of scope.content: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope of this Service. Specifically:

Obligatory.content: The following services are mandatory for the delivery of this service:

Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptio.content: The licenses and/or subscriptions listed in this section and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Cross-Functional Services are included in the Service and are not listed below. License description/remarks (no Cegeka owned licenses) -

Customer owned Licenses and/or subscript.content: The licenses and/or subscriptions listed in this section and their cost are not included in this Service. It is the customers responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. License description/remarks ODI licenses These are ‘passthrough’ licenses, meaning that the customers orders the licences via a formal PO and are annually charged.

Service delivery location.content: This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) Office environment.

Service Reporting.content: The Cross-functional reporting is part of this Service Reporting where it is applicable for this service (see section ‘Relation to Cross-Functional Services’). There are no specific (recurrent) reports for this Service.

Data classification.content: Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service. Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service. In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes).

Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer.content: This service is delivered with: Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues): Oracle support Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party): (none)

Adherence to Cross-Functional Services.content: The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in  the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services Anti-Malware management This service is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Anti-Malware management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. Vulnerability management & compliancy management (hardening) This service is not subject to vulnerability & compliancy management. Vulnerability & compliancy management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. All Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.

Service Window.content: The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.

Service Level.content: Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. Availability The following Availability KPI’s are provided for the Service: KPI ID KPI name Availability Service Window Metric KPI.0.138.Bronze TAM Bronze Bronze % availability in Service Window KPI.0.138.Silver TAM Silver Silver % availability in Service Window KPI.0.138.Gold TAM Gold Gold % availability in Service Window  The availability measurement is monthly and calculated as:

Standard Service Billing.content: The service is billed on a monthly basis. The Service is billed per billing element:

Change Request Billing.content: Changes are billed on a monthly basis. Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact. Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.
